Thuja subcuneata, also known as Mountain White Cedar, is a small to medium-sized evergreen tree native to Asia, characterized by its conical shape and aromatic foliage.

The health benefits of Thuja subcuneata include its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiviral properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, soothe respiratory issues, and support immune function.

The bioactive constituents of Thuja subcuneata include terpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and contribute to its fragrance.

The most common herbal preparations made with Thuja subcuneata include essential oils, teas, and tinctures, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory infections, skin issues, and digestive problems.
